goal: Context Reliability v1: anti_patterns + status (current/superseded/abandoned/stale) + guidance + followups in mainline context output

summary.title: Context Reliability v1: anti_patterns + 4-state retrieval status + per-intent guidance
summary.what: Implements Step 2 from docs_for_ai/mainline-spec-v0.2.md. Adds AntiPattern as a first-class type on IntentSummary (hard constraints, distinct from soft risks); introduces a retrieval-status enum (current/superseded/abandoned/stale) distinct from the lifecycle status; classifies stale via wall-clock age (≥90 days) OR file-churn (≥3 strictly-later intents touching the same file); pins superseded intents below their superseders in ranking; surfaces per-intent guidance as a single deterministic line per status; renames top-level Guidance→Notes to clear the namespace for the per-intent field. Bumps the agent guidance template to v9 with a four-status reading guide and the anti_patterns vs risks distinction. SealResult validation rejects anti-patterns with empty why and non-canonical severity. Six new tests pin the six invariants from the agent.md design protocol.
summary.why: Spec §2 says Mainline's product thesis is 'agent reads intent before code'. The retrieval API was the load-bearing surface for that thesis but had three quality gaps: (a) no way for a sealed intent to record hard constraints distinct from soft warnings — risks were free-form, agents could weigh them away; (b) the Status field returned raw lifecycle states (merged/proposed/etc) which don't tell the agent how to USE the intent right now; (c) supersedes ranking was a 0.85 multiplier that could let an old high-signal intent outrank its replacement. Step 2 closes all three.
summary.user_goal: When a coding agent runs `mainline context`, the result must (i) carry every hard constraint future agents must respect, (ii) tell the agent in one word whether to trust each intent right now, (iii) point at the replacement when an intent has been superseded.

decisions.chose: Two distinct fields on IntentSummary; risks remain free-form; anti_patterns are structured (what/why/severity) and rejected at seal time if why is empty.
decisions.rationale: The whole reason this distinction exists is so agents treat one as soft and the other as hard. Sharing a field would re-introduce the original problem.

decisions.chose: Replace the existing Status='merged'/'abandoned'/etc with a 4-value retrieval-only enum: current/superseded/abandoned/stale.
decisions.rationale: The retrieval consumer (agent) does not need lifecycle resolution. They need 'should I trust this now?' which is exactly what the 4 values answer. Lifecycle status is still in `mainline show`.

decisions.chose: Wall-clock age ≥90 days OR ≥3 strictly-later intents touched any of its files.
decisions.rationale: Two signals catch different staleness modes: rarely-touched code that just aged out, and hot files that have churned around an old decision. Both register as 'verify before applying'.

decisions.chose: Hard rule: if A is in the result and its superseder B is also in the result, A.score = min(A.score, B.score - 0.01).
decisions.rationale: The previous 0.85 multiplier could let a high-signal old intent outrank its replacement. The hard rule makes Property 3 (superseder strictly above) deterministic regardless of raw signal.

decisions.chose: guidanceFor(status, supersededBy) is a switch on 4 values; never derived from heuristics.
decisions.rationale: Determinism makes the agent's behaviour predictable AND keeps the wording centralized when product copy needs to change.

decisions.chose: Decisions and risks have top-N caps for readability; anti_patterns get copied through verbatim.
decisions.rationale: Property 5 — the load-bearing safety property is 'agent saw the constraint before editing'. Truncation breaks that property.

decisions.chose: The per-intent field gets `guidance`; the top-level rename is `notes`.
decisions.rationale: Same JSON name twice in a single response is confusing. Notes more accurately describes the top-level (per-result reminders) vs guidance (per-intent advisory).

rejected.alternative: Filter abandoned intents from retrieval entirely.
rejected.reason: Spec §9 lists `abandoned` as one of the four retrieval-status values. An abandoned intent IS valuable signal ('do not retry this approach'). Hide-by-default would lose that. Existing test (TestContextRetrieval_AbandonedIntentVisibleButPenalised) already pins this contract.

rejected.alternative: Make AntiPattern just a structured Risk (one field, two shapes).
rejected.reason: Soft vs hard is the whole point of the distinction. Mixing them in one field means downstream consumers (Hub, lint, eval) all have to re-derive 'is this a hard constraint?' from a substring match. Two fields, two semantics.
Reason text lives in the abandon event, not in StatusEvidence. Wiring that through is its own change. For now the abandoned guidance line + `mainline show` cover the case.

rejected.alternative: Apply the supersedes multiplier (1.0/0.85) approach instead of the score-pinning rule.
rejected.reason: Multiplier is non-deterministic in ranking outcome — depends on raw signal magnitudes. Score-pinning gives Property 3 unconditionally.

risks: The 90-day stale threshold is a heuristic. Repos that move slowly (libraries, infrastructure code) may flag everything stale; repos that move fast may flag nothing. Tunable later.

Score-pinning for supersedes uses a 0.01 epsilon; if the rounding boundary drives the score to 0, we cap at 0. Pathological case: superseder also at 0 — both rendered, order non-deterministic. Acceptable: the agent sees both, status field still tells the truth.
AntiPattern is a write-time addition; existing sealed intents don't have any. Retrieval falls through quietly when AntiPatterns is nil.

anti_patterns.what: Treating ContextRelevant.Status as the lifecycle status
anti_patterns.why: Status here is the RETRIEVAL status (current/superseded/abandoned/stale) — distinct from domain.IntentStatus. Future readers must call `mainline show` for the lifecycle status.

anti_patterns.what: Truncating anti_patterns when output budget is tight
anti_patterns.why: Property 5 — agents that don't see a hard constraint cannot avoid violating it. Truncate decisions/risks tail; never anti_patterns.

anti_patterns.what: Adding a field to IntentSummary without updating ValidateSealResult
anti_patterns.why: ValidateSealResult is the only seal-time gate. New required fields without a check there reach storage as empty strings and break retrieval invariants downstream.

followups: Step 3: SealResult lint v1 (richer than just ValidateSealResult — check decisions have rationale, risks vs anti_patterns appropriate split, etc).
Step 4: agent guidance keeps evolving — eval harness will tell us which sections need to be sharper.
Step 5: agent eval harness with the 8 fixtures listed in spec §9, especially the 'auth migration anti_pattern preservation' fixture which tests this exact surface.
Tune sharesFileMinOverlap and the stale heuristics once dogfooded across a few more repos.
Surface anti_patterns in `mainline show` text output too (currently only in `mainline context`).

architectural_claims: AntiPattern is a separate first-class type from Risk; the soft/hard distinction is enforced at write (validation) and read (no truncation) time.
Retrieval status is a 4-value enum distinct from lifecycle status; the agent reads only the retrieval status when deciding how to USE an intent.
Property 3 (superseder strictly outranks superseded) is enforced by score-pinning, not by ranking heuristic — deterministic regardless of raw signal.
behavioral_changes: ContextRelevant.Status now takes one of four retrieval-only values (current/superseded/abandoned/stale) instead of raw lifecycle status.
ContextRetrievalResult.Guidance renamed to Notes; ContextRelevant gains its own Guidance string (per-status one-liner).
Anti-patterns recorded at seal time appear unconditionally in `mainline context` output; rejected at seal time when `why` is empty.
Stale intents now visible with status=stale and an explicit verify-before-applying guidance line.
`mainline context` text output renders anti-patterns prominently, before decisions/risks.

api_changes.signature: engine.ContextRelevant.Status semantics changed: was lifecycle status, now retrieval status (current/superseded/abandoned/stale)

data_model_changes.name: IntentSummary on-disk shape
data_model_changes.migration_notes: AntiPatterns is omitempty; existing intents read back with nil and pass through retrieval cleanly.

data_model_changes.name: agents-md template version
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Bumped to v9. mainline agents update migrates in place; user content outside the markers preserved.

migration_notes: Run `mainline agents update` to pick up the v9 template (anti_patterns documentation + retrieval status guide).
Agents and tooling consuming `ContextRetrievalResult.Guidance` must rename to `.Notes`; consuming `ContextRelevant.Status` for raw lifecycle values must call `mainline show <id>` instead.
